name: 509 Cuisine
tagline:
Bold Caribbean flavors from the heart of Haiti.
Brought to Denver with love and tradition.
about_title: A Taste of Haiti in Colorado
about_paragraphs: 509 Cuisine brings the bold, vibrant flavors of Haiti to the Denver metro area. Every dish is a celebration of Caribbean tradition — slow-cooked, deeply seasoned, and made with soul.
From our signature griot and pikliz to our tender stewed chicken, each plate tells the story of Haitian home cooking passed down through generations.
Named after Haiti's country code, 509 is more than a restaurant — it's a piece of home for the Haitian community and a flavor adventure for everyone else.
menu_subtitle: Bold Caribbean flavors, made fresh daily

gallery_images.alt: Haitian plate with djon djon rice, plantains, and pikliz
Red snapper in Creole sauce with rice and plantains
Traditional Haitian platter with orchid garnish

items.name: Griot
items.desc: Fried pork marinated in citrus & spices

items.name: Poule en Sauce
items.desc: Stewed chicken in rich Creole sauce

reviews_list.text: The flavors were absolutely authentic — each dish felt like a trip to Haiti! The griot was perfectly crispy and the pikliz had just the right kick.
reviews_list.author: Google Reviewer

reviews_list.text: Finally, real Haitian food in Denver! The portions are generous, the rice is perfect, and the staff treats you like family. Will be back weekly.
reviews_list.author: Local Regular

reviews_list.text: 509 Cuisine is a hidden gem. The goat was tender and perfectly seasoned. If you haven't tried Haitian food before, this is the place to start.
reviews_list.author: First-time Visitor
